submission_xml.group_contribution: For the entire group, I have contributed to point 2. Use and integration of external API's, part 3. Configuration file and the logical er model. Being in a group of 3 people, we tried to divide the work equally, but it was pretty difficult to do that, especially when not everyone knew how to use sql to create a database, or creating php files. I have more experience using front-end and creating sql's to I took most of the coding & sql parts.

individual_component.type: Document the code and the system
individual_component.description: While creating most of the code, and even going through files which I haven't created, I have maped out what each sequence does from that part of the code. Although I haven't put explanations everywhere, as in I didn't say for javascript, every php file has deep details for it.
individual_component.problems: I have encountered problems when creating the weather.php file as it was kinda a new thing for me. The problems were relatively normal, as in the API key wasn't connecting, it wasn't gathering the correct information. And finally, it wouldn't find the coordinates
individual_component.solutions: The solution were that instead of trying to read the API Key, instead, I made it only for config.php so that the weather.php file summon it and connect to it. And I also made it so that it gathers the correct information by making it dirrectly connect to places_of_interestID from the database. Finally, to fix the final issue, I made it to gathered the lat and lon specifically from the database.

file.name: weather.php
file.purpose: To fetch and summon all weather critiria.

file.name: index.php
file.purpose: Main page. It's got both of the maps and weather forecast, but it does not have the places of interest. Those are for their own pages

submission_xml.individual_comment: This part was done by me and was even easier. It was all just so easy. Surprisingly so. Sed eu nunc ipsum, nec suscipit lectus. Quisque quis nibh at sem dictum pretium. Pellentesque augue mauris, lobortis vel elementum quis, consequat tempor felis.
